summary: Ukrainian forces reported numerous drone and missile attacks across multiple fronts, including a large-scale Shahed drone barrage and Iskander-M launch from Bryansk. Russian air defenses claimed to have downed 127 Ukrainian drones overnight. Ukraine announced assistance to Romania for air defence and continued coordination with NATO allies.
key_points: Ukrainian air defence downed 127 Ukrainian drones over Russian regions overnight (Source: @tass_agency/378193).
A massive attack involving 290 Shahed‑type drones, 5 H‑101 missiles and an Iskander‑M ballistic missile was reported, with 284 enemy targets destroyed (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
Russian‑controlled drones struck oil facilities in Taganrog, Armavir, and Feodosia, igniting a tanker and fuel depot (Source: @eRadarrua/94277).
Ukraine offered air‑defence support to Romania after a drone incident near Galaţi (Source: @UkraineNow/81540).
Two Iskander‑M missiles were launched from Bryansk region toward Ukrainian territory (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67301).
Russian Ministry of Defence reported a coordinated strike on Ukrainian military airfields and energy infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/378209).
Ukrainian special forces claimed to have hit 23 enemy targets, including oil depots, during night operations (Source: @UkraineNow/81552).

sections.executive_summary: A massive Shahed drone barrage combined with an Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch from Bryansk resulted in 284 enemy targets being destroyed, highlighting intensified aerial combat. Russian forces reported downing 127 Ukrainian drones overnight, while Ukrainian strikes hit oil facilities in Taganrog, Armavir, and Feodosia. Ukraine pledged air‑defence assistance to Romania amid cross‑border drone incidents.

points: Ukrainian air‑defence units reported shooting down 127 Ukrainian‑origin drones over Russian territory during a single night, according to the Russian Ministry of Defence (Source: @tass_agency/378193).
Ukrainian special operations forces claimed to have engaged 23 enemy targets, including two oil depots in Taganrog and Feodosia, during night‑time operations (Source: @UkraineNow/81552).
Ukraine announced it will support Romania's air‑defence against Russian drones, with Ukrainian specialists to assist (Source: @UkraineNow/81540).

Ukrainian forces reported multiple Shahed‑type drone incursions across the north, south, east and central regions, with at least seven drones observed near Kyiv and other oblasts (Source: @eRadarrua/94297, @kpszsu/63335).
Ukrainian military statements indicated successful strikes on Russian‑occupied oil and gas infrastructure in Kharkiv, Sumy, Poltava, and Dnipropetrovsk regions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30536).
The Russian Ministry of Defence announced a coordinated strike on Ukrainian military airfields, energy and transport infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/378209).
Russian authorities in Rostov region reported two individuals, including a teenager, charged with preparing a terrorist act (Source: @tass_agency/378212).
Russia's defence ministry claimed the downing of 127 Ukrainian drones over Russian regions overnight (Source: @tass_agency/378193).
Russian officials warned that the Romanian drone incident should not be publicized, suggesting EU countries remain participants in the war (Source: @tass_agency/378218).
Russian state media reported that the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant remains a potential high‑risk target, though no damage was confirmed (Source: @tass_agency/378228).
Shahed‑type drones were observed moving from Sumy toward Poltava and from Kharkiv toward Dnipropetrovsk, indicating sustained pressure on the eastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
Ukrainian reports noted a reactive UAV over Chernihiv region originating from Bryansk, suggesting cross‑border incursions (Source: @eRadarrua/94259).
Multiple UAV sightings were recorded near the border of Kharkiv and Sumy, with trajectories toward the front‑line towns of Krasnohrad and Izium (Source: @eRadarrua/94257).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on a fuel depot in Armavir, Krasnodar Krai, indicating Russian use of assets in the eastern theater (Source: @eRadarrua/94277).
A group of UAVs from Sumy moved toward Poltava, potentially targeting logistics hubs (Source: @kpszsu/63328).
UAVs were reported moving from Chernihiv toward Nizhyn and from the north of Kyiv toward Zhytomyr, indicating activity along the Belarusian border (Source: @kpszsu/63286, @kpszsu/63335).
A drone was observed near the city of Kyiv at 17:57, triggering an air‑raid alert (Source: @eRadarrua/94307).
Ukrainian sources noted UAVs flying along the northern border of Sumy toward Prokopivka (Source: @kpszsu/63319).
Reports indicated UAV activity over the border region of Rivne heading toward Volyn (Source: @kpszsu/63336).
Ukrainian forces observed a UAV near the border area of Chernihiv, moving along the Dobrjanka corridor (Source: @eRadarrua/94262).
UAV attacks targeted oil infrastructure in Taganrog and a tanker in the port, as well as a fuel depot in Feodosia, Crimea (Source: @eRadarrua/94277).
Ukrainian reports listed UAVs moving from the south of Dnipropetrovsk toward Vasylivka (Source: @kpszsu/63326).
A missile strike was reported on Vasylkiv/Bila Tserkva area, indicating continued missile activity in the southern direction (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67271).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV over Zaporizhzhia region, near the nuclear power plant, though no damage was confirmed (Source: @tass_agency/378228).
Ukrainian air‑defence noted a missile launch from the southern direction targeting the Kherson area, though details were limited (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67263).
A large‑scale aerial attack involved 290 Shahed drones, 5 H‑101 missiles and an Iskander‑M ballistic missile, with Ukrainian forces claiming 284 enemy targets destroyed (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
Russian forces reported downing 127 Ukrainian drones overnight across multiple regions (Source: @tass_agency/378193).
Ukrainian air‑defence systems intercepted two cruise missiles (X‑101) launched from Vologda region (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
Ukrainian radar detected a UAV over Kyiv prompting an air‑raid alert at 17:57 on 30 May (Source: @eRadarrua/94307).
Russian officials claimed a UAV was controlled via fiber‑optic cable, dismissing accidental incursion theories (Source: @tass_agency/378227).

key_developments: A coordinated strike involving 290 Shahed‑type drones, five H‑101 cruise missiles and an Iskander‑M ballistic missile resulted in 284 enemy targets being destroyed across Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/63300).
Russian Ministry of Defence reported downing 127 Ukrainian drones over Russian territory during a single night (Source: @tass_agency/378193).
Ukrainian forces struck oil facilities in Taganrog, Armavir and Feodosia, igniting a tanker and fuel depot (Source: @eRadarrua/94277).
Two Iskander‑M missiles were launched from Bryansk region toward Ukrainian positions (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67301).
Ukraine pledged air‑defence assistance to Romania following a drone incident near Galaţi (Source: @UkraineNow/81540).
Russian Ministry of Defence announced a group strike on Ukrainian military airfields and energy infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/378209).
Ukrainian special operations reported 23 enemy targets hit, including oil depots, during night operations (Source: @UkraineNow/81552).
sections.threat_assessment: The intensity of aerial engagements has escalated, with large‑scale drone swarms and ballistic missile launches indicating a shift toward high‑volume, multi‑vector attacks. Russian air‑defence claims of downing over a hundred Ukrainian drones suggest improved interception capabilities, yet Ukrainian UAV operations continue to target critical infrastructure in occupied territories. The cross‑border drone incident involving Romania underscores the expanding geographic scope of the conflict and the risk of NATO involvement.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is actively seeking and providing regional air‑defence cooperation, exemplified by its pledge to assist Romania and ongoing coordination with NATO allies. Russian statements warning EU countries to remain silent reflect heightened diplomatic sensitivities surrounding cross‑border incidents. International aid, such as Japan's financial support for non‑lethal equipment, indicates continued external backing for Ukrainian resilience.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths reveals persistent activity along Belarusian, Russian, and Crimean borders, suggesting coordinated launch sites. The use of fiber‑optic control for drones points to advanced Russian UAV capabilities. Continued strikes on oil and fuel facilities aim to disrupt Russian logistics, while the high volume of Shahed drones may strain Ukrainian air‑defence resources.
